user_request: Prepare a detailed plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as a hand loan by bank transfer. There is no written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice. Plead limitation properly. Claim interest at 12 per cent per annum from date of loan till realization. Draft strictly in accordance with Order VII CPC. Include proper cause of action, jurisdiction, valuation, court fee and prayer clause. Use formal Indian court language suitable for filing before City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

facts.summary: Recovery suit for Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as hand loan via bank transfer without written agreement. Transaction supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. Defendant defaulted despite oral demands and legal notice. Interest claimed at 12% per annum from date of loan till realization.

Order 7, r 1 of the code prescribes what a plaint should contain, but no section or order or Rule of CPC lays down 
as to what a petition should contain. On the other hand the Code does not say that the ingredients required for 
plaint are the ingredients for a petition. Therefore, a petition is completely different from a plaint. Thus, a petition for 
divorce under s 13 of the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 is not a plaint and a final order passed under the Act is not a 
decree under s 2 (2) of the CPC. Therefore, decree passed without separate prayer for consequential relief is 
enforceable and court-fees for divorce petition is not Rs. 20/- but Re. 1/- as prescribed for petition.33

An Act to consolidate and amend the law for the limitation of suits and other proceedings and for 
purposes connected therewith.

the case may be;

The starting point of limitation begins from the date when the decree becomes enforceable. Thus, where the 
judgment and order of the High Court on appeal by decree-holder was set aside by Supreme Court and the 
decree of the trial Court was reversed, the very cause of act ion which had accrued for restitution vanished. 
Finally the decree of trial Court was set aside on 31-10-1969 which gives rise to a fresh cause of action for 
restitution. Therefore, the limitation for filing application for restitution would start from that date and not from 
any anterior date. Consequently the restitution application was held to be within limitation.38

By the amending Act 104 of 1976, Act 127 of the Limitation Act, 1963 was amended providing for 60 days instead 
of 30 days as the period of limitation for filing an application to set aside a sale on execution of a decree, including 
any such application by a judgment debtor. A five judge bench of the Supreme Court, overruling the decision inP K 
Unni v. Nirmala Industries 113 observed
Viewed in this context the intention of the legislature in extending the period under Act 127, Limitation Act may be 
seen. It is very clear from the statement of objects and reasons, which have been set out herein above, that the 
period under art 127, Limitation Act was extended from 30 days to 60 days in order to give more time to persons to 
make deposits. The legislature has noted that the period of 30 days from the date of sale was too short and often 
caused hardships because judgment-debtors usually failed to arrange for moneys within that period.114
The Bench further observed (Variava J, speaking for the court) as follows in para 18 of the judgment:
Having given our careful consideration to the question, we are of the opinion that there is no anomaly and that there 
are no different periods of limitation for making deposits and/or filing an application for setting aside the sale. It is by 
virtue of O 21, r

THE LIMITATION ACT, 1963 
ACT NO. 36 OF 1963 
[5th October, 1963.] 
An Act to consolidate and amend the law for the limitation of suits and other proceedings and for 
purposes connected therewith. 
BE it enacted by Parliament in the Fourteenth Year of the Republic of India as follows:— 
PART I 
PRELIMINARY 
1. Short title, extent and commencement.—(1) This Act may be called the Limitation Act, 1963. 
(2) It extends to the whole of India 1***. 
(3) It shall come into force on such date 2 as the Central Government may, by notification in the 
Official Gazette, appoint. 
2. Definitions.—In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires,— 
(a) “applicant” includes— 
(i) a petitioner; 
(ii) any person from or through whom an applicant derives his right to apply; 
(iii) any person whose estate is represented by the applicant as executor, administrator or 
other representative; 
(b) “application” includes a petition;

draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
{{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
R/O {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
... Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
{{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
R/O {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
... Defendant

PLAINT
FILED UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHEWETH AS FOLLOWS:

1. That the Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by profession, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and is a person of sound mind and competent to sue.

2. That the Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by profession, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and is a person of sound mind and competent to be sued.

3. That the present suit is being filed for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) advanced as a hand loan to the Defendant, together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of advance till the date of realization.

4. That the valuation for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fees is Rs. {{TOTAL_VALUATION}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_VALUATION_WORDS}} only) comprising the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- and interest accrued thereon up to the date of filing. The requisite court-fees of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid.

5. That sometime in the month of {{MONTH_OF_LOAN}}, {{YEAR_OF_LOAN}}, at {{PLACE_OF_LOAN}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and requested the Plaintiff to advance a friendly hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal/business exigencies. Pursuant to such request and in good faith, the Plaintiff agreed to advance the said loan amount to the Defendant.

6. That in furtherance of the said oral agreement and on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the account of the Defendant bearing account number {{DEFENDANT_ACCOUNT_NUMBER}} maintained with {{BANK_NAME_AND_BRANCH}} through NEFT/RTGS/IMPS/Bank Transfer, as evidenced by the bank statement bearing reference number {{TRANSACTION_REFERENCE_NUMBER}}.

7. That no written agreement, promissory note or any other document was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction, and the entire transaction was based purely on mutual trust and oral understanding. The said oral agreement is not required by law to be reduced to writing, and the absence of a written deed is not fatal to the plaint, the terms being gatherable from the conduct of the parties and the attendant circumstances.

8. That as per the oral agreement, the said loan was repayable on demand by the Plaintiff. The Plaintiff, from time to time, made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the aforesaid loan amount, the last of such oral demands being made on or around {{DATE_OF_LAST_ORAL_DEMAND}}. Despite such oral demands, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the said amount.

9. That on {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}, the Plaintiff caused a detailed legal notice to be issued to the Defendant through {{MODE_OF_SERVICE_OF_NOTICE}}, calling upon the Defendant to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum within fifteen days from the date of receipt thereof. The said notice was duly received by the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE_BY_DEFENDANT}}, as evidenced by the acknowledgment receipt.

10. That notwithstanding the service of the said legal notice and the expiry of the time stipulated therein, the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to repay the aforesaid loan amount or any part thereof. The Defendant is hence liable to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest thereon at the agreed rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization.

11. That the cause of action for filing the present suit first arose on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE_BY_DEFENDANT}} when the Defendant received the legal notice and failed to comply with the same, and subsequently on {{DATE_OF_REFUSAL_AFTER_NOTICE}} when the Defendant expressly/impliedly refused to repay the amount after the expiry of the notice period. The cause of action is continuing and is accruing de die in diem till the date of realization of the amount.

12. That the present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 6 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (for money payable on demand) or in the alternative under Article 113 thereof (residuary article), having been filed within three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action.

13. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the Defendant resides and/or carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and/or the cause of action wholly or in part arose at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}} within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court is also satisfied as the value of the subject-matter of the suit is Rs. 15,00,000/- (exclusive of interest).

14. That the Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
(a) Bank statement dated {{DATE_OF_BANK_STATEMENT}} evidencing the transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's account;
(b) WhatsApp communications/screenshots between the Plaintiff and Defendant dated {{DATES_OF_WHATSAPP_MESSAGES}} corroborating the loan transaction and subsequent demands;
(c) Legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} issued by the Plaintiff to the Defendant;
(d) Acknowledgment of receipt of the said legal notice;
(e) True copies of letters/emails, if any.

15. That the Plaintiff has not filed any other suit, civil or criminal, against the Defendant in respect of the cause of action giving rise to the present suit.

WHEREFORE, in the facts and circumstances stated above, the Plaintiff humbly prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only);

(b) Pass a further decree for interest on the said principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization;

(c) Pass a decree for the costs of the suit;

(d) Grant such other relief or reliefs as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

BENGALURU
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}                                                    ({{PLAINTIFF_NAME}})
                                                                            Plaintiff

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, son/daughter of {{PLAINTIFF_FATHER_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, by occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:

1. That I am the Plaintiff in the above-mentioned suit and I am conversant with the facts of the case.

2. That the contents of paras 1 to {{LAST_PARA_NUMBER}} of the preceding plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge, and the contents of para {{EVIDENCE_PARA_NUMBER}} are based on legal advice and the records maintained by me, which I believe to be true.

3. That I have not suppressed any material fact.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}} day of {{MONTH_OF_VERIFICATION}}, {{YEAR_OF_VERIFICATION}}.

({{PLAINTIFF_NAME}})
 Plaintiff

ANNEXURES
Annexure-A: Copy of Bank Statement dated {{DATE_OF_BANK_STATEMENT}} evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/-.
Annexure-B: Copies of WhatsApp Communications between the Plaintiff and Defendant.
Annexure-C: Copy of Legal Notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}.
Annexure-D: Copy of Postal Receipt/Acknowledgment of service of Legal Notice.

IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_FULL_NAME}},
age {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},

age {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, occupation {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
r/o {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
... Defendant

PLAINT
FILED UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHEWETH AS FOLLOWS:

1. That the Plaintiff is an adult Indian citizen, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, by occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, and is competent to sue in these proceedings.

2. That the Defendant is an adult Indian citizen, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, by occupation {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, and is competent to be sued. The present address of the Defendant is the place where the Defendant resides and/or carries on business, and is within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

3. That the Plaintiff seeks the recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) advanced as a friendly hand loan to the Defendant, together with interest thereon at the rate of 12 per cent per annum from the date of advance till the date of realization.

4. That the valuation of the subject-matter of the suit for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fees is Rs. {{TOTAL_VALUATION}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_VALUATION_WORDS}} only), comprising the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- and interest accrued thereon up to the date of filing. The requisite court-fees of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on the plaint.

5. That on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, at {{PLACE_OF_TRANSACTION}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and requested the Plaintiff to advance a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) as a hand loan for the personal/business exigencies of the Defendant. The Plaintiff agreed to advance the said amount to the Defendant based on mutual trust and an oral understanding between the parties.

6. That in pursuance of the said oral agreement and on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the bank account of the Defendant bearing account number {{DEFENDANT_ACCOUNT_NUMBER}} maintained with {{DEFENDANT_BANK_NAME_AND_BRANCH}} through electronic bank transfer, as evidenced by the bank statement bearing transaction reference number / UTR {{TRANSACTION_REF_NUMBER}}.

7. That no written agreement, promissory note, or any other document was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction. The entire transaction was based purely on an oral agreement, mutual trust, and the conduct of the parties. The absence of a written deed is not fatal to the claim, as the terms of the oral agreement are gatherable from the circumstances, conduct of the parties, and the bank transfer effected pursuant thereto.

8. That as per the oral agreement between the parties, the said loan was repayable on demand by the Plaintiff. The Plaintiff, from time to time, made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the aforesaid loan amount, the last of such oral demands being made on or about {{DATE_OF_LAST_ORAL_DEMAND}}. Despite such oral demands, the Defendant failed, neglected and refused to repay the said amount or any part thereof.

9. That on {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}, the Plaintiff caused a detailed legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} to be issued to the Defendant through {{MODE_OF_SERVICE_OF_NOTICE}}, calling upon the Defendant to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum within fifteen days from the date of receipt thereof. The said notice was duly received by the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}, as evidenced by the {{ACKNOWLEDGMENT_TYPE}}.

10. That notwithstanding the service of the said legal notice and the expiry of the time stipulated therein, the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to repay the aforesaid principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- or any interest thereon. The Defendant is thus liable to repay the principal amount together with interest at the agreed rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization.

11. That the cause of action for filing the present suit first arose on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} when the Defendant received the legal notice and failed to comply therewith, and subsequently on {{DATE_OF_REFUSAL}} when the Defendant refused to repay the amount after the expiry of the notice period. The cause of action is continuing and is accruing de die in diem till the date of realization of the amount.

12. That the present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 6 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, for suits for money payable on demand, having been filed within three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action, i.e., from the date of refusal after demand.

13. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit. The Defendant resides and/or carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which is within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The cause of action wholly or in part arose at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}}, which is also within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court is satisfied as the value of the subject-matter of the suit is Rs. 15,00,000/- (exclusive of interest).

14. That the Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
(a) Bank statement dated {{DATE_OF_BANK_STATEMENT}} evidencing the transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's account;
(b) WhatsApp communications/screenshots between the Plaintiff and Defendant dated {{DATES_OF_WHATSAPP_MESSAGES}}, the contents of which are proved in accordance with Section 65B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872;
(c) Legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} issued by the Plaintiff to the Defendant;
(d) {{ACKNOWLEDGMENT_TYPE}} evidencing receipt of the said legal notice by the Defendant;
(e) True copies of the correspondence, if any.

15. That the Plaintiff has not filed any other suit, civil or criminal, against the Defendant in respect of the same cause of action.

WHEREFORE, in the facts and circumstances stated above, the Plaintiff humbly prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only);

(b) Pass a further decree for interest on the said principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization;

(c) Pass a decree for the costs of the suit;

(d) Grant such other relief or reliefs as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

BENGALURU
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}                                                    ({{PLAINTIFF_FULL_NAME}})
                                                                            Plaintiff

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_FULL_NAME}}, son/daughter/wife of {{PLAINTIFF_PARENT_SPOUSE_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, by occupation {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:

1. That I am the Plaintiff in the above-mentioned suit and I am conversant with the facts of the case.

2. That the contents of paragraphs 1 to 15 of the preceding plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge, and the contents of paragraph 14 are based on legal advice and the records maintained by me, which I believe to be true.

3. That I have not suppressed any material fact.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}} day of {{MONTH_OF_VERIFICATION}}, {{YEAR_OF_VERIFICATION}}.

({{PLAINTIFF_FULL_NAME}})
 Plaintiff

ANNEXURES
Annexure-A: Copy of Bank Statement dated {{DATE_OF_BANK_STATEMENT}} evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/-.
Annexure-B: Copies of WhatsApp Communications between the Plaintiff and Defendant.
Annexure-C: Copy of Legal Notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}.

3. That the present suit is being filed for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) advanced as a hand loan to the Defendant, together with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of advance till the date of realization.

4. That the valuation for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fees is Rs. {{TOTAL_VALUATION}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_VALUATION_WORDS}} only) comprising the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- and interest accrued thereon up to the date of filing. The requisite court-fees of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid.

5. That on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, at {{PLACE_OF_LOAN}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and requested the Plaintiff to advance a friendly hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) for his personal/business exigencies. Pursuant to such request and in good faith, the Plaintiff agreed to advance the said loan amount to the Defendant.

6. That in furtherance of the said oral agreement and on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the account of the Defendant bearing account number {{DEFENDANT_ACCOUNT_NUMBER}} maintained with {{BANK_NAME_AND_BRANCH}} through Bank Transfer, as evidenced by the bank statement bearing reference number {{TRANSACTION_REFERENCE_NUMBER}}.

7. That no written agreement, promissory note or any other document was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction, and the entire transaction was based purely on mutual trust and oral understanding. The said oral agreement is not required by law to be reduced to writing, and the absence of a written deed is not fatal to the plaint, the terms being gatherable from the conduct of the parties and the attendant circumstances.

8. That as per the oral agreement, the said loan was repayable on demand by the Plaintiff. The Plaintiff, from time to time, made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the aforesaid loan amount, the last of such oral demands being made on or around {{DATE_OF_LAST_ORAL_DEMAND}}. Despite such oral demands, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the said amount.

9. That on {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}, the Plaintiff caused a detailed legal notice to be issued to the Defendant through {{MODE_OF_SERVICE_OF_NOTICE}}, calling upon the Defendant to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at 12% per annum within fifteen days from the date of receipt thereof. The said notice was duly received by the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE_BY_DEFENDANT}}, as evidenced by the acknowledgment receipt.

10. That notwithstanding the service of the said legal notice and the expiry of the time stipulated therein, the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to repay the aforesaid loan amount or any part thereof. The Defendant is hence liable to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest thereon at the agreed rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization.

11. That the cause of action for filing the present suit first arose on {{DATE_OF_RECEIPT_OF_NOTICE_BY_DEFENDANT}} when the Defendant received the legal notice and failed to comply with the same, and subsequently on {{DATE_OF_REFUSAL_AFTER_NOTICE}} when the Defendant refused to repay the amount after the expiry of the notice period. The cause of action is continuing and is accruing de die in diem till the date of realization of the amount.

12. That the present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 6 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (for money payable on demand), having been filed within three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action.

13. That this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the Defendant resides and/or carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and/or the cause of action wholly or in part arose at {{PLACE_OF_CAUSE_OF_ACTION}} within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court is also satisfied as the value of the subject-matter of the suit is Rs. 15,00,000/- (exclusive of interest).

14. That the Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
(a) Bank statement dated {{DATE_OF_BANK_STATEMENT}} evidencing the transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's account;
(b) WhatsApp communications/screenshots between the Plaintiff and Defendant dated {{DATES_OF_WHATSAPP_MESSAGES}} corroborating the loan transaction and subsequent demands;
(c) Legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} issued by the Plaintiff to the Defendant;
(d) Acknowledgment of receipt of the said legal notice;
(e) True copies of letters/emails, if any.

15. That the Plaintiff has not filed any other suit, civil or criminal, against the Defendant in respect of the cause of action giving rise to the present suit.

WHEREFORE, in the facts and circumstances stated above, the Plaintiff humbly prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only);

(b) Pass a further decree for interest on the said principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of loan, i.e., {{DATE_OF_LOAN_TRANSACTION}}, till the date of realization;

(c) Pass a decree for the costs of the suit;

(d) Grant such other relief or reliefs as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.
